name: Precious Metals Refinery
description_short: Precious Metals Refinery buys gold, silver, jewelry, coins, diamonds, and luxury watches in Phoenix, Arizona with instant evaluations and competitive payouts.
description_long: Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) is a precious metals and jewelry buyer operating locations in Phoenix and Tucson, Arizona, and Palm Desert/Palm Springs, California. The company specializes in purchasing gold, silver, platinum, coins, diamonds, engagement rings, and luxury timepieces from consumers looking to liquidate items or clear estates. PMR positions itself as a local alternative to national chains, emphasizing personalized service and transparent transactions. The Phoenix location on Indian School Road is managed by Olga Cottone and operates six days per week (Monday–Saturday, 10 AM–6 PM). PMR offers more than just buying services—they provide jewelry and watch repair, custom jewelry design, and in-home consultations for large collections. Their team includes GIA-certified gemologists, coin specialists (numismatists), and watch experts who evaluate items using industry-standard tools and practices. The company explicitly promises price-matching in the Phoenix/Scottsdale/Tempe area and invites customers into private buying offices so valuables are never out of sight during the transaction. All evaluations are free and presented as no-pressure offers. Distinguishing features include their claims of transparent processes, secure/confidential handling, and competitive rates backed by real-time analysis. PMR also provides educational content on inherited jewelry and precious metals value, positioning themselves as expert advisors rather than transactional buyers. However, as a buyer of precious metals and secondhand luxury goods, PMR is fundamentally a pawn shop or collateral-based liquidation service rather than a lender. Consumers should understand that this is a one-way transaction (selling items for cash), not a loan product, and the "top dollar" pricing claims are marketing—actual offers depend on current spot prices, condition, authenticity, and the company's assessment of resale value.
pros: Free expert evaluations by GIA-certified gemologists and numismatists with no obligation to sell
Transparent buying process—customers accompany valuables into private offices and see real-time analysis
Multiple service offerings beyond buying (jewelry/watch repair, custom design, in-home consultations)
Price-match guarantee for competing offers in Phoenix, Scottsdale, and Tempe area
Six-day weekly availability (Monday–Saturday, 10 AM–6 PM) and same-location transaction completion
Specialization in luxury watch brands (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe) with expert appraisal
Estate and bulk collection handling with in-home consultation option for high-value items
cons: As a buyer, not a lender—customers receive cash for items sold, not loans against collateral (one-way transaction)
Pricing dependent on current spot prices, condition assessment, and company's resale value estimates; 'top dollar' claims are not independently verified
Limited geographic footprint (only Phoenix, Tucson, Palm Desert, and Palm Springs locations)
No transparency on buyback policies, holding periods, or dispute resolution if customer questions valuation after sale
Custom jewelry design and repair services may carry separate costs and timelines not detailed on website
best_for: Consumers liquidating inherited jewelry or estates and seeking expert appraisals without sales pressure
Owners of luxury watches or designer jewelry (Tiffany, Cartier, David Yurman, Van Cleef & Arpels) needing specialist evaluation
Individuals with large collections or high-value items preferring in-home evaluation for convenience/security
Phoenix-area residents seeking local price-matching on gold, silver, coins, and precious metals sales
diagnosis: Precious Metals Refinery is best for Phoenix-area consumers with gold, jewelry, coins, or luxury watches to sell who want expert appraisal and transparent evaluation in a local setting. The main caveat is that this is strictly a buying service (cash for items), not a loan or pawn arrangement—customers do not retain ownership or the ability to reclaim items after sale, and resale value is ultimately determined by PMR's assessment and current market prices.
services: Gold, silver, and platinum buying (bullion, jewelry, flatware)
Luxury watch buying and appraisal (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe)
Engagement ring and designer jewelry purchase (Tiffany & Co., David Yurman, Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels)
In-home consultations for large estates or bulk collections
Instant cash payouts upon sale completion
